name: Frozen Peanut Butter Pie with Caramel Sauce
recipeInstructions:
This frozen peanut butter pie has a crunchy chocolate crust and is served with warm caramel sauce.
For sauce: Melt butter in a heavy medium saucepan over medium heat. Add 1/2 cup whipping cream, both sugars, and corn syrup; bring to boil, stirring frequently. Reduce heat and simmer until slightly thickened (about 5 minutes). Mix in vanilla extract. Cool.
(Optionally, prepare the sauce up to this point ahead of time, cover, and chill.)
For crust: Spray a 9-inch-diameter glass pie dish with nonstick vegetable oil spray. Finely grind chocolate wafer cookies in a processor. Transfer cookie crumbs to a bowl. Add 5 tablespoons melted butter and stir until moist crumbs form. Press the crumb mixture onto the bottom and up the sides of the prepared dish. Place the crust in the freezer.
For Filling: Using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese and sugar in a large bowl until smooth. Beat in peanut butter. Using an electric mixer fitted with clean dry beaters, beat whipping cream and vanilla extract in a medium bowl to stiff peaks. Fold the whipped cream into the peanut butter mixture. Mound the filling onto the chilled crust. Smooth the top. Freeze overnight.
For Glaze: Bring whipping cream to a simmer in a heavy medium saucepan. Add chopped semisweet chocolate and stir until melted and smooth. Cool to lukewarm. Spoon the glaze over the filling. Sprinkle with chopped peanuts. Freeze until the chocolate sets (about 30 minutes).
(Optionally, prepare the glaze ahead of time, keep frozen.)
Serve: Let the pie stand at room temperature for a few minutes before cutting. Using a warm knife, cut the pie into wedges. Place on plates. Bring the sauce to a simmer, thinning with more cream if necessary, and spoon around the pie.
